subject: Rheumatology
question: A 45-year-old woman presents with 2 months of bilateral hand and wrist pain with morning stiffness lasting >1 hour. Exam shows swelling of MCP and PIP joints. RF positive, anti-CCP positive. What is the best initial DMARD?

options.explanation: Methotrexate is first-line DMARD for RA. Start 15mg weekly, titrate to 25mg. Give with folic acid 1mg daily to reduce side effects (nausea, hepatotoxicity). Monitor CBC, LFTs, creatinine q3 months. Treat-to-target: remission or low disease activity.
Prednisone is bridging therapy while DMARD takes effect (8-12 weeks). NOT long-term monotherapy due to osteoporosis, diabetes, infection risk. Always combine with DMARD.
Hydroxychloroquine is for MILD RA or combination therapy. Not adequate alone for moderate-severe RA with positive RF/anti-CCP (poor prognostic factors).
Biologics (anti-TNF) are second-line after MTX failure. Start with MTX first. Biologics reserved for inadequate response after 3-6 months of MTX.
NSAIDs treat symptoms only, do NOT prevent joint destruction. DMARD therapy essential for disease modification. NSAIDs are adjunctive, not primary therapy.

educationalObjective: Initiate methotrexate as first-line DMARD for RA. Add folic acid. Treat-to-target with regular monitoring.
keyTakeaway: RA: MTX first-line + folic acid. Monitor CBC/LFTs. Anti-CCP + RF = poor prognosis. Biologics if MTX fails. Target: remission.
